title: Uthar Wynn
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Character |type=Sith |image=[[File:Uthar_Wynn_Render.jpg]] |name=Uthar Wynn |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[3956 BBY]],''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' in the [[Tomb of Naga Sadow]] on [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]]''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' |species=[[Humanoid/Legends|Humanoid]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes=[[Color/Legends|Yellow]] {{C|[[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]]}} |skin=Light |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]] **[[Sith Academy (Korriban)|Sith Academy]] |masters=[[Jorak Uln]] {{C|[[Sith Master/Legends|Sith Master]]}} |apprentices=*[[Yuthura Ban]] {{C|[[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]]}} *[[Dustil Onasi]] {{C|[[Sith acolyte/Legends|Sith Acolyte]]}} *[[Selene]] {{C|Sith Acolyte}} }} {{Quote|Unless victory proves your superiority, it is an illusion. It is temporary.|Uthar Wynn|audio=Uthar4-KotOR.flac|}} '''Uthar Wynn''' was a [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Sith Master/Legends|Sith Master]] and the [[Headmaster/Legends|Headmaster]] of the [[Sith Academy (Korriban)|Sith Academy]] on the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Planet/Legends|necropolis]] [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]] during the [[Jedi Civil War]]. Wynn was [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprenticed]] to the Academy's former Headmaster [[Jorak Uln]], whom he eventually drove out of the Academy, although Wynn did not [[Death/Legends|kill]] Uln. Wynn then became the Academy's Headmaster, taking the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] female [[Yuthura Ban]] as his apprentice. In the Jedi Civil War's [[3956 BBY|final year]], the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]]—who was the [[Jedi training/Legends|reconditioned]], amnesiac former [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]]—traveled to Korriban in search of the [[Star Forge]], an ancient [[Rakata/Legends|Rakatan]] [[Space station/Legends|space station]] under the control of Revan's former apprentice and the reigning Dark Lord, [[Darth Malak]]. Revan gained the approval for entrance into the Academy from Wynn's apprentice Ban, and the Headmaster assigned Ban as Revan's teacher. Wynn awarded Revan by giving him prestige for completing various tasks at the Academy, although the Jedi managed to avoid the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side's]] corruption. After Revan gained enough prestige, Wynn and Ban took the Jedi to the ancient [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow's]] [[Tomb of Naga Sadow|tomb]]. As part of Revan's final test, Wynn tasked him with reaching the Rakatan [[Star Map]] in the tomb, which Revan was searching for, and retrieving a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]. Wynn ordered Revan to kill Ban after the Jedi found the Star Map and acquired the lightsaber, but Revan sided with the Twi'lek. Wynn attacked both his Sith apprentice and the redeemed Jedi, but was ultimately killed by them. ==Biography== ===Early life=== {{Quote|I strived many years to hone my power until I was able to drive out my predecessor and rule the academy.|Uthar Wynn|audio=Uthar3-KotOR.flac|}} [[File:JorakUln-K1.jpg|thumb|left|175px|Wynn's Sith Master and former Headmaster of the Korriban Sith academy, Jorak Uln]] Uthar Wynn, a [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], studied at the [[Sith Academy (Korriban)|Sith Academy]] on the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Planet/Legends|necropolis]] [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]] under its Headmaster, [[Jorak Uln]], who had served with [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun's]] [[Sith Empire (Exar Kun)/Legends|Sith Empire]] during the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]. Wynn eventually gained enough power to drive his [[Sith Master/Legends|Sith Master]] out into the [[Valley of the Dark Lords/Legends|Valley of the Dark Lords]], where Uln would remain, studying inside the [[Tomb of Tulak Hord|tomb]] of the ancient [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Tulak Hord]]. Wynn then assumed control over the Academy and took a former [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] female [[Yuthura Ban]], as his [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]]. Wynn devoted his attention to running the Academy itself and put Ban in charge of selecting the strongest [[Sith hopeful|hopefuls]] for training. ===Jedi Civil War=== {{Quote|We are as the Force is meant to be. The Jedi would hide that from you… they would tell you the dark side is too quick, too easy, all so that they need never challenge the passions that lie within them.|Uthar Wynn|audio=Uthar2-KotOR.flac|}} At some point during the [[Jedi Civil War]]—which had been initiated by the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lords]] [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] and [[Darth Malak]]—[[Dustil Onasi]], the son of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] [[Commander/Legends|commander]] [[Carth Onasi]], was convinced by a [[Human/Legends|Human]] female named [[Selene]] to study at the Sith Academy on Korriban. During his time at the Academy, Dustil became a promising student. He developed a relationship with Selene, who, compared to Dustil, was not as powerful of a student. This distressed Wynn, who felt that such an emotional entanglement would hinder Dustil's progress. The Sith Master secretly had Selene [[Death/Legends|murdered]], believing Dustil to be too promising of a student. Wynn then lied to Dustil about her death, saying Selene had died in the Valley of the Dark Lords.''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' {{C|[[Uthar Wynn's datapad|Datapad]] containing Wynn's orders}} Near the end of the of Jedi Civil War, Wynn's apprentice, Ban, began plotting to take control of the Academy. Wynn soon noticed Ban's ambitions and planned to have her removed. In [[3956 BBY]], the final [[Time/Legends|year]] of the war, the amnesiac former [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] Revan, now a [[Jedi training/Legends|retrained]] Jedi, came to Korriban in his search for a [[Star Map]] that led to the [[Star Forge]], an ancient [[Rakata/Legends|Rakatan]] [[Space station/Legends|space station]] under the control of Revan's former apprentice and the current Dark Lord, Malak. In order to access the Star Map, Revan and his companions gained entry into the Academy and were brought before Wynn, along with the students [[Lashowe]], [[Mekel]], and [[Shaardan]]. The Headmaster welcomed the students to the Academy and spoke briefly about the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]]. He informed the students that the first of them to gain enough prestige would be worthy of becoming a Sith, and then asked each student if they were ready to learn the dark side's ways. When Lashowe answered, Wynn told her that she was brash and fiery, as he had expected, and implored Lashowe to convert her passion into an advantage. Mekel replied that he was ready, and Wynn told the student that he sensed his anger and expressed his belief that Mekel's anger would provide him power. Shaardan said that he was always ready to learn, and Wynn warned Shaardan that he would not survive the coming trials if he did not "gather his wits." During his conversation with Revan, Wynn told the Jedi that his companions were irrelevant, believing that Revan's allies were his [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]]. Wynn informed the students that the first one of them who learned and recited the [[Code of the Sith/Legends|Code of the Sith]] to him would be rewarded with prestige. The Headmaster then assigned Ban as the student's teacher, while they attempted to prove themselves worthy of what Wynn considered a full Sith. After learning the Sith Code from Ban, Revan went to Wynn and finished the Code as the Sith Master spoke it, correctly giving the last word for each phrase of the code. Wynn also asked Revan a question pertaining to the code, which Revan answered correctly, impressing the Sith Master. {{GameLSmechanics|Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic}} Revan broke into Wynn's room in the Academy and stole his [[Uthar Wynn's datapad|personal datapad]], which contained his order to kill Selene. He gave Wynn's datapad to Dustil, causing the apprentice to abandon the Sith ways and leave the Academy. Revan and Ban conspired with each other to kill Wynn at his final trial in the ancient Dark Lord [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow's]] [[Tomb of Naga Sadow|tomb]]. However, Revan informed Wynn of Ban's treachery in order to gain more prestige. The Headmaster gave Revan a [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]]—which contained instructions to [[Poison/Legends|poison]] Ban—to give to the Sith [[Adrenas]], promising that Revan would have the opportunity to kill Ban in the final test if he gained enough prestige. Rather than give the datapad to Adrenas, Revan gave the device to Ban and informed the Twi'lek that Wynn plotted to kill her. She instructed Revan to break into Wynn's chambers and place a small device under his bed, which would poison Wynn during his sleep and weaken him for the final trial. The Jedi did as instructed. Revan recovered a [[Sith holocron/Legends|Sith holocron]] near the Sith Lord Tulak Hord's tomb with the assistance of the Sith student Lashowe. She promised to tell Wynn that he had helped her; she did not, however, and when Revan asked Wynn about this, the Sith Master pointed out that prestige could not be shared. Wynn ordered Revan and the students to locate and execute the Sith apprentice [[Thalia May]] and her fellow renegade students. Revan helped May and the renegades escape Korriban instead and deceived Wynn into believing that he had killed the students. After recovering the Sith Lord [[Ajunta Pall]]'s [[Sword of Ajunta Pall|sword]] from his [[Tomb of Ajunta Pall|tomb]], Revan killed the Sith student Shaardan after the Sith attacked him in order to claim the sword for himself.{{CSWECite|III|142|Shaardan}} Wynn received Pall's sword from Revan, who also told the Sith Master that he had eliminated Shaardan. Wynn awarded Revan prestige for both these feats. The Sith Master also ordered the destruction of a [[Mark VII experimental prototype assassin droid|rogue assassin droid]] that was hidden in the ancient Sith Lord [[Marka Ragnos]]' [[Tomb of Marka Ragnos|tomb]]. Revan helped the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] escape Korriban and lied to Wynn about the droid's destruction. Revan encountered Wynn's former Sith Master, Jorak Uln, in Tulak Hord's tomb. After being [[Torture/Legends|tortured]] by Uln, Revan killed the former Headmaster and presented Wynn with Uln's [[Jorak Uln's Sith tablet|research]]. {{Endgame}} ====Death==== [[File:UtharvsYuthura2.jpg|thumb|right|220px|Wynn duels Yuthura Ban in Naga Sadow's tomb.]] {{Quote|So the time has come, has it? I… I will… ugh… no! My… strength leaves me!!''"
"''You are weak, Uthar, and the Force has abandoned you. We have made sure of it!|Uthar Wynn and Yuthura Ban|audio=UtharYuthura-KotOR.flac|}} Revan eventually gained enough prestige over his fellow students, while at the same time maintaining the tenets of the [[Jedi Code/Legends|Jedi Code]], to avoid being corrupted by the dark side, and Wynn deemed Revan ready to become a full Sith. For Revan's final test, Wynn and Ban took Revan to Naga Sadow's tomb. Wynn instructed him to find the Star Map and bring back a ceremonial Sith [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]. After Revan successfully completed his task, Wynn congratulated Revan. The Sith Master told Revan that he needed to kill someone whom he had become familiar with in order to become a complete Sith and instructed Revan to strike down Ban. However, Revan, who had befriended Ban during his stay at the Academy, sided with the Twi'lek. Enraged, Wynn tried to attack Ban and Revan, but the poison had taken effect and weakened him. Wynn's Sith apprentice and the Jedi slew the Headmaster. {{GameLSmechanics}} After Wynn's death, Ban turned on Revan, but the Jedi managed to defeat her.{{CSWECite|III|337|Wynn, Uthar}} Revan then convinced Ban to return to the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], and Ban forsook the dark side, abandoning the Academy. After Revan returned to the Academy, the Sith instructors, [[Troop/Legends|troops]], and apprentices [[Skirmish on Korriban|attacked]] Revan and his companions when Wynn did not return with him, although Revan and his companions managed to defeat the Sith. {{Endgame}} ===Legacy=== {{Quote|More recently, this tomb was where Revan confronted Uthar Wynn, the leader of the Sith Academy. When Revan left Korriban, the Sith Academy was thrown into turmoil—with their leader gone, many fought for the right to rule. And so the Sith here turned on each other, resulting in the carnage you can see covering the surface of this valley.|Kreia, to Meetra Surik|audio=Uthar5-KotORII.flac|''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''}} After Malak's [[Battle of Rakata Prime|death]] at Revan's hands, the remaining Sith on Korriban [[Sith Civil War|fought]] over the right to rule the Sith. [[3951 BBY|Five years]] after the end of the Jedi Civil War, the Human female [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] known as [[Meetra Surik]]''[[The Old Republic: Revan]]'' traveled with the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Kreia]] to Korriban in search of Jedi Master and former [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Council]] member [[Lonna Vash]]. Kreia [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathically]] guided Surik through the Valley of the Dark Lords. When Surik approached Naga Sadow's tomb, Kreia informed Surik that after Revan defeated Uthar Wynn in the tomb and left Korriban, the Academy fell into turmoil. With their leader gone, the Sith fought each other for the right to rule, resulting in the skeletal remains littered throughout the Valley and the Academy lying in ruins. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|We, however, do not treat the Force as a burden. We treat it as a gift, a thing to be celebrated. We use it to acquire power over others… and why should we not? Because the Jedi say we should not?|Uthar Wynn|audio=Uthar1-KotOR.flac|}} [[File:Uthar.jpg|thumb|left|175px|Wynn in the Korriban Sith Academy]] Uthar Wynn was a bald humanoid male whose head was adorned with [[Tattoo/Legends|tattoos]]. His eyes were [[Color/Legends|yellow]] due to his immersion in the dark side. Wynn possessed certain traits that were typical of the Sith, such as an intolerance of failure, and believed that those who could not defeat their opponents deserved their fate. Wynn was not above having some of his students killed if it would allow another student to progress further without delay, such as the situation involving Dustil Onasi and Selene. He admired acts of betrayal and deception, but not when he was on the receiving end. Wynn did not view [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] as a burden, instead considering it as a gift. He believed that the Force should be used to acquire power over other individuals and felt that the Sith were what the Force was meant to be. He also believed that an opportunity was to be taken advantage of, even if it was not guaranteed to be successful. To Wynn, victory was temporary and an illusion, unless the victory proved one's superiority. He was not opposed to slavery; when Revan asked the Sith Master about his companions, Wynn said that Revan's "slaves" were irrelevant, as long as they did not interfere with Revan's or anyone else's training. When asked by Revan about his past, Wynn only replied that he strove to hone his power for years until he betrayed his Master, Jorak Uln. Wynn possessed a calm demeanor; however, when betrayed by his enemies, he brought out his rage. Although it was rumored that mentioning Wynn's former Master, Uln, in the Sith Master's presence was not a good idea, Wynn in fact no longer cared about his old Master's fate. Wynn was gullible to the extent that he believed Revan when he lied to Wynn about defeating Thalia May and the renegade students whom the Headmaster had ordered killed, despite the fact that Revan had actually allowed the renegade students to escape. Wynn also did not sense deception from Revan when the Jedi lied to Wynn about destroying the rogue assassin droid. Wynn believed that Naga Sadow was the greatest of Korriban's deceased Dark Lords. Wynn, like Uln, possessed a [[Double-bladed lightsaber/Legends|double-bladed]] [[Uthar Wynn's lightsaber|lightsaber]] containing a [[Color/Legends|red]] [[Lightsaber crystal/Legends|crystal]], which he used in [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|combat]]. He also owned a [[Sith mask/Legends|Sith mask]], a set of Jedi Master [[Jedi apparel/Legends|robes]], a [[Cinnagar war suit]], and a personal datapad. Rather than wearing a set of robes, Wynn wore a [[Sith officer]]'s uniform. ==Powers and abilities== [[File:Uthar-in-action.png|thumb|right|200px|Uthar Wynn brandishing his double-bladed lightsaber.]] Being a Sith Master, Wynn was adept at the use of the dark side, wielding powers that enabled him to [[Telekinesis/Legends|choke]] his enemies. He was also able to use the dark side to [[Force drain/Legends|drain]] his enemy's life-force and draw it into himself in order to replenish his own vitality. Wynn was capable of using the Force to [[Force resistance|render himself invulnerable]] to most offensive [[Force power/Legends|Force powers]] for a limited period of time. Having possessed a double-bladed lightsaber, Wynn was skilled in its use. ==Behind the scenes== {{Quote|I am Darth Revan, bow before me!''"
"''Ah, I see. So it is, Darth Revan, not dead as we were led to believe. And yet, you are *not*Revan, are you? What has occured between the time of your reported death and now, I can only guess at, but you've changed much. Regardless, your destiny takes you away from here, to confront your old pupil, without a doubt. Yes, that's what lies before you. Go to your destiny, then, my dark lord. I shall say nothing of what has happened here. If you prove to be successful and wish to return, then I, and all Sith, shall be your servants|Master Wynn pledges his loyalty to Revan|}} Uthar Wynn was created as a villain for the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'', developed by [[BioWare]].{{WebCite|url=http://www.ign.com/games/star-wars-knights-of-the-old-republic/pc-16512|text=Star Wars Knights of the Old Republic|archivedate=20161027055618}} There are multiple choices for the player to make in ''Knights of the Old Republic'' regarding Uthar Wynn's fate. Revan could side with Ban and kill Wynn. Once completed, Ban would turn on Revan. After her defeat, Ban would plead for mercy and Revan would spare her life and allow her to assume the position of the Academy Headmaster. A second choice involves Revan not allying with either Wynn or Ban; both Wynn and Ban attack him and are killed; as a consequence, though, Revan and his party will be attacked by everyone in the Academy, and be forced to fight their way out. A third option has Revan siding with Wynn and attacking Ban. Revan then allows Wynn to live and go back to lead the Academy. The fourth possible outcome has Revan not siding with Wynn, instead attacking and killing him with Ban's help. Ban then attacks Revan; when Revan defeats Ban, she pleads for mercy. Revan apologizes to Ban and kills her. A fifth choice has Revan choosing not to side with either Wynn or Ban, claiming he was the only true Sith of the three, and killing both. Revan can also kill Ban for Wynn and then tell the truth about why he sought the Star Map, stating that he never intended to become a Sith. Wynn instantly becomes enraged and attacks Revan, stating that he would "tear the flesh from his bones." Wynn would also attack if Revan did not tell him that Ban was plotting to kill him.If Korriban is the final world that a dark-sided Revan visits, Revan can tell Wynn that he is Darth Revan after defeating the Headmaster. Wynn recognizes Revan, secretly devotes his allegiance to him, and vows to pledge the Academy to Revan should he successfully destroy Darth Malak. ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' {{1st}} *''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'' {{Mo}} ==Sources== *[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]] *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Mediacat|soundcat=Audio files of Uthar Wynn|imagecat=Images of Uthar Wynn}} {{Reflist}} ==External links== *{{SWTORWiki}} {{Interlang |cs=Uthar Wynn |de=Uthar Wynn#legends |es=Uthar Wynn |pl=Uthar Wynn }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Wynn, Uthar}} [[Category:Humanoids]] [[Category:Individuals of unidentified species]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Sith Masters]] [[Category:Sith of Revan's Sith Empire]]
